question: Explain how an absorption at about 1700 cm-1 helps to identify a compound.
full_answer: An absorption at about 1700 cm-1 indicates a carbon oxygen double bond. This means the compound contains a carbonyl group.

question: Explain why a chloro compound shows both an M peak and an M+2 peak.
full_answer: Chlorine has two common isotopes 35Cl and 37Cl. Molecules containing 37Cl have a mass two units greater than molecules containing 35Cl. So both an M peak and an M+2 peak are seen.

question: Explain why absorptions at 2850–2950 cm-1 are not useful for deciding between compounds A, B and C.
full_answer: These absorptions are due to C-H bonds. All of the possible compounds contain C-H bonds, so this region does not distinguish between them.

question: Explain why a compound containing chlorine shows an M and an M+2 peak.
full_answer: Chlorine has two common isotopes, 35Cl and 37Cl. Molecules containing 37Cl have a mass two units higher than molecules containing 35Cl. Therefore both an M peak and an M+2 peak are seen.

question: Explain why a compound is identified as a ketone from its infrared spectrum.
full_answer: The spectrum has a strong absorption around 1700 cm-1 showing a C=O bond. There is no broad absorption due to O-H. So the compound can be identified as a ketone.

question: Explain why a molecule containing one chlorine atom has an M peak and an M+2 peak in the mass spectrum.
full_answer: Chlorine has two common isotopes, 35Cl and 37Cl. Molecules containing 37Cl have a mass two units greater than molecules containing 35Cl, so an M+2 peak is observed as well as the M peak.

question: Explain why a compound containing one chlorine atom shows molecular ion peaks at M and M+2.
full_answer: Chlorine has two common isotopes, 35Cl and 37Cl. Molecules containing 37Cl have a mass two units higher than molecules containing 35Cl. Therefore both M and M+2 peaks are seen.

question: Explain why a compound containing a carbonyl group shows a strong infrared absorption around 1700 cm-1.
full_answer: The C=O bond vibrates and absorbs infrared radiation at around 1700 cm-1. This absorption is strong because the carbonyl bond is highly polar. Therefore a strong peak appears in this region.

question: Explain what is meant by the molecular ion peak in a mass spectrum.
full_answer: The molecular ion peak is due to the ion formed when one electron is removed from a molecule without fragmentation. It therefore represents the mass-to-charge ratio of the molecular ion.
